notes: 100 Mbps token-ring LAN standard; prominent as campus backbone technology at time of study.
Layer-2 frame switching for 10 Mbps Ethernet segments; evolved through Fast, Gigabit, 10GbE, etc.
Cell-based switching technology positioned as future backbone. OC-3 (155 Mbps) UNI targeted for LANplex 2500 uplink in Q1 1996.
100 Mbps over twisted-pair copper; uplink card for LANplex 2500 expected Q1 1996.
3Com proprietary ASIC delivering 565K pps forwarding. Front-end engine to RISC processor, creating 'fast path' architecture.
3Com's Roving Analysis Port implemented distributed RMON across all ports. Cisco supported RFC 1271.
3Com's 3-stage networking vision: Stage 1=collapsed backbone, Stage 2=distributed LAN switches, Stage 3=ATM migration.
16-port 10 Mbps Ethernet + 2 FDDI ports. Modular workgroup switch. ISE-chip ASIC. Introduced December 1994.
3Com flagship backbone switch. Market leader in backbone switching at time of study. Larger FDDI concentration capacity than 2500.
16-port Ethernet switch with single FDDI port. LANplex technology repackaged in 3Com SuperStack form factor for SMB market.
3Com ATM workgroup and backbone switch family. Separate from LANplex family.
Flagship network management application. Supported HP OpenView, Sun NetManager, and IBM NetView platforms. Windows version expected Q4 1995.
LANplex 2500 supported VLANs via port groups and MAC address groups. Cited as key user requirement.
3Com proprietary buffering approach: guaranteed minimum buffers per port, dynamic expansion as needed. Reduces packet drops under contention.
IEEE 802.3u 100 Mbps Ethernet standard, ratified 1995; successor to 10BASE-T, preceded Gigabit Ethernet.
